title: Working with Help Texts
text: Working with Help Texts Define help texts to create large blocks of text that can be displayed in a Uniface help window. Note: Help texts are provided for backward compatibility. They are not recommended for modern applications. Open the help text editor: Choose More Editors > Help Texts . Select or create the Message Library in which you want to store the help texts. The object editor is now opened. To create a help text: Click New and enter the Help Name , Language , and optional Description . Note: Each help text must have a unique name. It cannot use the same name as a message because messages and help texts are stored in the same library. Enter the text in the Text field. Optionally, set the Window Geometry to define the position and size of the help window. To define the position, set the Left and Top properties. To define the dimensions, set the Width and Depth (Height) properties. Figure 1. Help Text Editor To delete a help text, select the help text and choose Edit > Delete Help Text . To save new or modified help texts, choose File > Save . To call a Uniface help text: Use the help and $text ProcScript commands to specify the help text to be called. Ensure that the appropriate language and library are defined for the help text, either in ProcScript or in the assignment file. trigger help $variation = myapp $language = usa help $text(HLP523) end; help Copy Related Links Messages Messages are language-related objects that enable you to deploy a Uniface application in different languages and locales. They are stored in language-specific libraries, and Uniface automatically chooses the appropriate library at runtime based on the configuration. help Display help information, either a Uniface help message or information from the native help system. $text Return the text of a message or help text. $textexist Return an indication of whether the specified message or help text exists.
